1.0:
It is now an era many years after 
the Demon Lord was defeated by the Hero.
1.1:
The once famous Hero and Sage,
their legends have now become nothing more
than fairy tales adults use to scold children.
1.2:
This is the name given to the mysterious giant labyrinth
that suddenly appeared on the surface ten years ago.
1.3:
Its seemingly endless depth,
areas filled with various ruins,
and monsters rapidly breeding under its magical power.
1.4:
With the Demon Lord no longer threatening humanity,
the sudden birth of this unknown labyrinth,
instantly shattered the brief peace of the human world.
1.5:
The Adventurers’ Guild organized many exploration teams 
to delve into it, but they never made any decisive progress.
However, the labyrinth has never shown any intention of invading 
or attacking the outside world.
1.6:
Local lords and various powers,
all tried to name and manage the labyrinth.
1.7:
Yet all those names were forgotten over time,
and in the end, only 'Abyss' remained,
deeply etched into people’s memories.
1.8:
Since the labyrinth showed no signs of 
expanding to consume the world,
the Adventurers’ Guild finally decided——
to open its exploration rights to the public.
1.9:
Thus, the once forgotten profession,
'adventurer',
welcomed a new era of glory and fervor.
1.10:
And in this final great labyrinth,
I have only one goal——
to find a way to completely cure my \str[120]’s condition———
4.0: My father was a brilliant alchemist.
4.1:
Even in this peaceful era where there’s no need to fight demons,
he still actively solved several alchemical mysteries.
4.4:
Yet this man, admired by all,
was now kneeling weakly on the ground,
repeatedly apologizing
to the young me.
4.5:
His grip on my shoulders tightened,
and I couldn’t help but start crying.
Perhaps realizing he’d lost control,
he slowly loosened his hands and gently patted my head instead.
4.6:
"It’s okay now… it’s Daddy’s fault,
I shouldn’t give up so easily…"
4.7:
"You’re a good child, 
you shouldn’t have to bear all this…
leave the rest to Daddy."
4.8: "That’s right… even if it costs me everything… I must…"
4.9:
His voice started to tremble,
was he scared of something?
4.10:
I didn’t understand any of it then, 
but I remember that after that day,
he never returned to being the kind, gentle father I once knew.
5.0:
\nl<Man>"The feathers dropped by the Firebird...
Even when soaked in strong acid, they do not corrode...
If it is for that... we still need—"
5.1:
The man's voice echoed lowly in the workshop, 
which was crammed with ancient tomes.
Various alchemical instruments were stacked neatly 
across the workbench.
5.2:
A sudden flurry of light, 
hurried footsteps interrupted the man's muttering.
A barefoot girl with snow white hair barged into the dusty,
potion scented chaos of the workshop without warning.
5.3:
\nl<Boy>\{"Ah...! Found you!!
Stop running off everywhere!!"
5.4:
Following close behind the girl was a slightly older boy.
He had clearly been chasing her for a long time. 
he was leaning over, hands on his knees, panting heavily as 
he pointed at the girl with an irritated shout.
5.6:
The girl, the subject of his scolding, simply stood there blankly.
Her hollow eyes reflected no one.
5.7:
\nl<Boy>"Honestly! That face again! not even a single peep!
Dad! I don't want to take care of her anymore!!"
5.8:
\nl<Boy>"It's been days and she hasn't opened her mouth once!
She just keeps wandering off on her own... 
this isn't fun at all!!"
5.9: \nl<Man>"Haaah... The same old story again, I see..."
5.10:
Watching the girl stare intently at the instruments 
while his son threw a tantrum, the man let out a long sigh 
and was forced to halt his work.
5.11:
\nl<Man>"You're quite the handful yourself, little brat.
How many times have I told you? 
Don't enter the workshop without my permission. It's dangerous!"
5.12:
\nl<Boy>"Hmph... 
says the useless adult who relies on a kid to make lunch!
When are you going to stop messing with this boring junk 
and teach me real alchemy?"
5.13:
\nl<Man>"Cough, cough...! 
Didn't I just transmute a toy sword for you the other day? 
Father has been busy identifying these relics brought up from 
the Abyss for the Guild. I really don't have time for your antics—"
5.14:
\nl<Boy>"That crappy sword broke ages ago! — The Abyss?
You mean that Grand Labyrinth that suddenly appeared!?
Hey, hey! When can I go take a look too?"
5.15:
\nl<Man>"Absolutely not! That's no place for a child's adventure!
We moved here specifically to conduct research and 
develop countermeasures. 
Just be patient...!"
5.17:
The girl, previously as still as a doll, seemed to sense something.
Her movements, precise as a machine, stirred once more.
She suddenly turned and dashed past the two of them, 
heading straight out of the workshop.
5.19:
\nl<Man>"Sigh... 
neither of you makes things easy... go catch her quickly!
And remember, do not let her leave the estate grounds! 
The barrier here isn't fully stable yet!"
5.20:
The boy wanted to argue back, 
but seeing the girl about to vanish from his sight,
he could only stomp his feet in frustration 
and take off after her again.
5.21:
\nl<Man>"..................Sigh.
I hope time... is still on our side."
5.22:
Standing by the door, the man watched the distant figures—
the boy finally catching up as the two of them scuffled together.
He whispered a murmur filled with heavy concern.
5.23:
Then, as if escaping some dark premonition, 
he sat back in his chair and buried himself 
in his research once more.
